fitScoreNote: Two years doing the exact job in the exact target vertical -- forward-deployed delivery in healthcare AI automation, discovery through production
jobDescriptionText:
Role: Technical Product Manager

About 8090
Co-founded and led by Chamath Palihapitiya, 8090 is building a Software Factory that delivers fully-managed and hosted software purpose-built for each customer, cutting through the bloated Software Industrial Complex.

The Opportunity
Lead the end-to-end creation and implementation of AI-first enterprise products, from customer discovery through successful delivery and ongoing engagement.

Key Responsibilities
- Lead customer discovery to document business processes and translate requirements into actionable items, maintaining the PRD throughout the project lifecycle
- Manage multi-stream, technical engagements by developing project governance plans, schedules, team goals, success criteria, and milestones
- Ensure goal alignment across stakeholder groups including customers, and internal teams
- Create and deliver tailored communications ranging from executive-level project updates to detailed iteration plans
- Track metrics and provide weekly status reports to demonstrate progress and value
- Represent customer business needs throughout the development process
- Plan, organize, and manage all phases of the project lifecycle to ensure successful delivery

What Sets You Apart
- Proven ability to deliver enterprise solutions on time and within budget, ideally involving AI/ML integrations
- Exceptional stakeholder management skills for high-impact client engagements
- Experience managing teams in fast-paced, resource-constrained environments
- Strong project management capabilities with a track record of successful delivery
- Domain expertise in regulated industries

Qualifications
- 5+ years of experience in Technical Product Management with emphasis on customer delivery
- Demonstrated success managing the full lifecycle of enterprise products
- Strong technical acumen with ability to communicate with both business stakeholders and engineering teams

Compensation: $150,000-$1,000,000 + stock/options + benefits.

strengths.title: Forward-deployed TPM in healthcare AI for two years
strengths.description: The 8090 model (discovery → PRD → execution → production) maps precisely to what I did at Thoughtful AI as Solutions Architect, Customer Engineer, and Lead TPM. Primary customer interface from first call through live deployment for 60%+ of Thoughtful's 2024 deliveries.

strengths.title: Production hybrid-RAG system with real billing workflow stakes
strengths.description: Built Thoughtful's dental eligibility system from scratch -- a hybrid-RAG pipeline hitting 95% accuracy on a live production billing workflow, maintained with a labeled holdout evaluation suite. Not a demo. Real billing teams relied on it daily.

strengths.title: Anticipates unforced errors before they become delivery debt
strengths.description: The multi-portal eligibility project at Thoughtful is the clearest case study: original timeline was 6 months for 20+ portals; actual pace was one portal every two months. I built a data model to quantify the gap, had honest conversations, and rebuilt trust. The automation estimation engine formalized this into a reusable scoping artifact.

strengths.title: Healthcare domain depth: 12+ EHR platforms, 48+ provider organizations
strengths.description: EDI/FHIR/HL7 fluency, HIPAA/SOC 2 documentation, RCM-specific workflow knowledge across 8 product lines. For a 32-person company, a TPM who does not need to ramp on the domain is a significant operational advantage.

strengths.title: Built a software factory for my own team (22 skills, 943 commits)
strengths.description: At Smarter Technologies I built a 22-skill, 5-agent AI operating system for the SE team -- essentially an internal software factory for sales engineering. I understand the power of AI-generated scaffolding and the human judgment required to keep it on rails. That instinct transfers directly to 8090's core product thesis.

weaknesses.title: 8090's product is a platform, not a service
weaknesses.description: My delivery experience is in services -- Thoughtful AI built custom automation for each customer. 8090's Software Factory is a platform that generates software customers then own.
weaknesses.mitigation: The TPM role bridges this. My job has always been owning the customer outcome, not any single delivery modality. Whether output comes from a handcrafted implementation or an AI-generated pipeline, the job is the same.

weaknesses.title: Verticals beyond healthcare are undocumented
weaknesses.description: Domain expertise is deep in healthcare RCM. 8090 targets four regulated verticals including financial services, manufacturing, and federal.
weaknesses.mitigation: The pattern recognition I built around EHR integration complexity, payer APIs, and compliance requirements directly parallels what you encounter in financial core banking or federal procurement. The framework transfers.

projects.name: Dental Eligibility Intelligence
projects.relevance: Production hybrid-RAG eligibility system for a healthcare billing workflow -- the type of AI delivery project 8090 TPMs oversee for enterprise clients.

projects.name: Notion Meeting Intelligence
projects.relevance: Multi-agent pipeline that turns sales meeting recordings into structured deal intelligence -- shows the agentic platform-building approach directly relevant to 8090's delivery model.
